world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 12
major_event: Caeris Thorne woke before midday with the memory of a dream still burning behind his eyes: himself, seated on the throne of Tidefall, the Admiral's seat empty beside him. He told no one. But he ordered his garrison commander to account for every soldier in the palace district before nightfall.

primary_event.name: The Proclamation Leaks — First Contact
primary_event.summary: The Gnome agent's servant contact assembled the full picture by mid-morning: two lords, a candle before dawn, a private chamber, and the Seer sleeping nearby. He sold the inference to the Gnome for three silver. By evening the Gnome Syndicate holds the most complete intelligence on the succession in the known world — and is deciding what to do with it.

supporting_events.name: Deep Entity: First Dream Beyond Lostfeld
supporting_events.summary: One of Ironmaul's separated soldiers, transferred to the outer garrison at the southern pass, reported the same blue-corridor dream to a Vilefin goblin scout who shared overnight shelter. The dream has now moved outside Lostfeld's walls for the first time. The Entity's reach is widening.

supporting_events.name: Admiral Countersigns — Retainer Clause Activated
supporting_events.summary: The Admiral's legal review concluded without flagging the Dur Khadur retainer clause. He countersigned the full harbor-treaty at midday, confident in his coalition. The clause is now legally active. Dur Khadur's advance unit at the coastal staging area has standing authorization to deploy.

supporting_events.name: Arkenor's Gamble — Velorn Responds
supporting_events.summary: The Velorn clan head summoned the Arkenor lieutenant in person. The meeting was brief and not friendly. Velorn now knows the immunity offer exists and knows Lythara sees him as a target, not an ally. He is mobilizing clan guards quietly. Lythara does not yet know any of this.

active_events.name: The Proclamation Leaks — First Contact
active_events.summary: The Gnome Syndicate now holds confirmed intelligence: a regency proclamation was signed before dawn on Day 11. They know the witnesses, the hour, and the Seer's proximity. They are considering three options: sell to Tidefall, sell to Caeris, or hold and leverage both.
active_events.consequences: Every tick the Gnomes delay is a tick the Admiral operates on false footing. If they sell to Tidefall first, the Admiral learns before the court and may suppress. If they sell to Caeris, he gains warning of who knows. The Vampires are one inference behind and closing.

active_events.name: Deep Entity: Dream Contagion Spreads
active_events.summary: The blue-corridor dream has passed outside Lostfeld's walls through an isolated soldier's contact with a goblin scout. The Entity communicates through sleep without regard for stone walls or separation orders. Kessa remains motionless. Varek is 1 tick away.
active_events.consequences: The dream as a contagion vector is now documented outside Lostfeld. If the Vilefin leadership interprets the soldier's account, it may spread into goblin networks. Varek's arrival becomes more urgent as the radius of influence expands.

active_events.name: Twin Cities Succession — Dual Instruments
active_events.summary: The proclamation is signed and now leaking. The letter of intent underpins the Admiral's entire coalition. Both are legally plausible. Caeris spoke to his garrison commander this morning, citing the dream as private motivation for increasing palace security. The commander complied without question.
active_events.consequences: Caeris is quietly hardening his physical position before any public declaration. When the proclamation becomes common knowledge, his garrison posture will already be in place. The Admiral does not yet know he is already legally behind.

active_events.name: Admiral Countersigns — Retainer Clause Active
active_events.summary: The harbor-treaty is signed. The Dur Khadur retainer clause gives the Dark Council autonomous deployment authorization in the event of 'succession uncertainty formally declared.' The clause is now legal and binding. The Admiral believes he has concluded a clean agreement.
active_events.consequences: Dur Khadur's advance unit can move the moment succession uncertainty is formally declared — by anyone. The Admiral does not control the trigger. He may not understand this until mercenary forces are already in position at Twin Cities approaches.

active_events.name: Rowen Opens the Iron Door — Destination Closing
active_events.summary: The fleet continues east. The sealed-order destination appears to be a coastal ruin on the far shore of Tidefall's eastern lanes. Captains know enough to navigate. Crew knows only that they are moving with purpose. Rowen has begun writing something in his cabin — not a log entry.
active_events.consequences: Whatever Rowen is writing may be insurance, testament, or instruction. If the destination is a ruin associated with the Hollow Accord's origins, arrival could produce evidence that reshapes what several factions believe about the present political order.

active_events.name: Grand Druid Varek — One Tick from Lostfeld
active_events.summary: Varek has cleared the Gnome passes and is now in the highland approach to Lostfeld. The Gnome transit watchers noted he carried nothing for trade and spoke to no one. One Gnome courier has been dispatched toward Lostfeld ahead of him — not to warn, but to observe the reception.
active_events.consequences: The Gnomes now have a watcher positioned to observe how Lostfeld receives Varek. If the Druid is admitted to the deep levels, the Gnomes will know something extraordinary is being contained below. This information is potentially more valuable than glass tolls.

active_events.name: Velorn Clan Mobilizes — Shadow Court Fracture Deepens
active_events.summary: Velorn clan guards are quietly repositioning within Faerwood. The clan head has not yet gone to Lythara — he is still processing. The Arkenor lieutenant's gamble has destabilized all three parties simultaneously. Nythariel watches and has sent no word to anyone.
active_events.consequences: If Lythara does not learn of Velorn's response within 1 tick, she will act on false information. Her next move against Velorn will be calibrated for a passive target, not an alerted and mobilizing one. The resulting overreach could fracture the Shadow Court visibly.

active_events.name: Caeris Thorne's Dream — Throne of Tidefall
active_events.summary: Caeris dreamed himself seated on the Tidefall throne — the Admiral's seat empty beside him. He woke unsettled, said nothing, and issued a garrison accountability order he told himself was unrelated. The dream's origin is unclear: personal ambition, Entity influence, or coincidence.
active_events.consequences: If the dream recurs or others share a version of it, it becomes a pattern. For now it is private and Caeris is the only witness. His garrison order, however, is a real and visible consequence that courtiers will begin to read.

faction_actions.action: The third Gnome agent assembled the full intelligence picture and dispatched a coded relay to Gnome Syndicate leadership before midday. He is now waiting for instruction on whether to sell the proclamation intelligence, leverage it, or sit on it. Cost: 3 gold (courier), 5 influence (activation of senior relay network).
faction_actions.reason: They hold the most valuable political intelligence in the succession. The correct play is not speed — it is positioning. They will not move until leadership replies, likely next tick.
faction_actions.target: Gnome Syndicate leadership / succession intelligence decision

faction_actions.action: Caeris Thorne quietly ordered his garrison commander to account for all soldiers in the palace district by nightfall. He framed it as routine security review. He spent the afternoon reviewing the proclamation document in private and drafting a conditional public announcement for when he judges the moment correct. Cost: 2 gold (administrative), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The dream shook him more than he admits. He is not acting on it directly, but hardening his position as if expecting pressure from an unexpected direction.

faction_actions.action: The Admiral countersigned the full harbor-treaty at midday with ceremony and two witnesses from his council. He dispatched a formal notification rider to Twin Cities announcing the completed agreement and inviting a reciprocal signature on the supplemental trade clauses. Cost: 4 gold (ceremony, rider), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: He believes he is concluding a clean alliance. He does not know about the proclamation or the retainer clause's implications. He is operating on 2-tick-old information.

faction_actions.action: Dur Khadur's Tidefall envoy received confirmation of the countersignature and immediately notified the Dark Council via fast rider. The advance unit at the coastal staging area was placed on readiness status — not deployment, but preparation. Cost: 5 gold (rider, unit readiness), 3 military.
faction_actions.reason: The clause is now active. They will not deploy until succession uncertainty is formally declared, but being ready before that declaration is the entire point of advance positioning.
faction_actions.target: Coastal staging area / retainer clause readiness

faction_actions.action: The Seer walked the palace grounds for the second morning and noticed the garrison accountability order being executed — soldiers being counted, positions logged. He did not understand the reason but recognized the shape: a man who signed something is preparing the ground around it. He began drafting a private note he has not yet decided whether to send. Cost: 1 influence (observation access).
faction_actions.reason: He does not know about the dream. He is reading the palace as a landscape and the garrison order reads as a quiet fortification. He suspects the proclamation is already more public than Caeris intended.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul received word from the southern pass garrison that one of the separated soldiers had spoken to a goblin during a shared shelter stop. He did not yet know the content — only that it happened. He recalled the soldier immediately and placed him under medical isolation. Cost: 2 gold (rider), 4 military (guard reassignment).
faction_actions.reason: He is sealing leaks he cannot fully understand. The recall is precautionary but too late for the information already transmitted. He is counting hours until Varek arrives.

faction_actions.action: Grand Druid Varek cleared the highland approach and made camp 4 hours from Lostfeld's outer gate. He sent a runner ahead to announce his arrival for tomorrow morning. He opened the sealed iron case for the first time since departure and reviewed its contents alone for two hours before resealing it. Cost: 2 gold (runner, camp supplies).
faction_actions.reason: He wanted to reconfirm his preparations before arrival. Whatever the case contains, he needed to see it again before meeting Ironmaul. The runner ensures he is admitted without delay.

faction_actions.action: The goblin scout who shared shelter with the dream-soldier returned to the forward camp and reported the strange account to the band leader. The leader added it to the sketch dispatch heading back to Rock Plains leadership: 'soldier from Lostfeld described descending corridor dream, blue light, cold.' Cost: 1 gold (courier addition).
faction_actions.reason: The goblin leadership does not yet know what to make of it, but any unusual intelligence from near Lostfeld is worth documenting. The fracture sketch and the dream account travel together.
faction_actions.target: Rock Plains goblin leadership / combined intelligence dispatch

faction_actions.action: Rowen spent the day writing in his cabin — not a log. The captains navigated east without incident. He emerged at dusk to confirm heading and returned below. One captain observed him speaking quietly to himself while reading a document that appeared older than parchment should be. Cost: 1 military (reduced watch command).
faction_actions.reason: He is processing what he read in the iron-doored chamber. The document he is consulting appears to be a reproduction of something from the chamber itself — notes he made while below, or a fragment he took.

faction_actions.action: Lythara sent a second message to the Arkenor lieutenant — this one less offer, more demand. She requested confirmation of his decision within one day or the offer would be withdrawn and replaced with consequences. She still does not know he disclosed the offer to Velorn. Cost: 3 influence (message security), 2 gold.
faction_actions.reason: She is reading his silence as negotiation delay. She is not reading it as betrayal because she does not yet have the information that would make betrayal legible. Her pressure escalation is walking toward a trap.

faction_actions.action: The Vampire operative who influenced the wavering lord pressed him for anything the lord had heard since the Seer's departure. The lord mentioned seeing two unfamiliar faces leaving through a servant passage before dawn two days ago — neither nobleman he recognized. The Vampire is now one inference from the proclamation. Cost: 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: They are assembling the picture through their social penetration. The servant passage detail is not proof but it narrows the window considerably. One more contact and they have enough.
faction_actions.target: Wavering Twin Cities lord / Dawn departure intelligence

faction_actions.action: Elowen's Wayfarer trader arrived at the Twin Cities outer market by late afternoon — one tick ahead of estimate due to a favorable road. He purchased a small quantity of grain at fair price and began carefully cultivating a cloth-merchant contact with connections to the inner court. Cost: 4 gold (grain, lodging, merchant relations).
faction_actions.reason: The trader is building commercial cover credibly before attempting any deeper intelligence access. Moving too fast would expose the mission's real purpose.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities inner court contact network

faction_actions.action: The Farrock rider received no response to the sealed report left with the Twin Cities court secretary. He sent a second note requesting acknowledgment and described the surface fracture details more explicitly — 'forty meters, warm air rising, northeastern orientation.' He will wait one more day before departing. Cost: 1 gold (note, messenger).
faction_actions.reason: He is establishing a documented paper trail. If the Twin Cities eventually connects the Lostfeld anomaly to a crisis, Farrock wants formal prior notice on record.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities court secretary / formal acknowledgment

faction_actions.action: A Gilgeth orc caravan operating the southern trade road passed through the Rock Plains and made contact with Vilefin Goblin traders. The orc caravan leader was visibly intoxicated on opioid compound and negotiated poorly — trading three weeks of surplus grain for a fraction of its value in goblin glass. Cost: 15 food (disadvantageous trade), 5 gold.
faction_actions.reason: The addiction-driven caravan behavior continues to destabilize orc economic capacity. The Vilefin profited significantly from the impaired negotiation.
faction_actions.target: Rock Plains trade corridor / grain-for-glass exchange

recent_events.text: The Gnome Syndicate's servant contact assembled the full picture of the pre-dawn signing and sold it. By evening, Gnome leadership holds confirmed intelligence on the proclamation. Caeris issued a garrison accountability order he claims is routine. The Seer noticed it and began drafting a note.

The Admiral countersigned the harbor-treaty with ceremony, unaware the Dur Khadur retainer clause is now legally active. A notification rider was dispatched to Twin Cities. Dur Khadur's coastal unit moved to readiness. The Admiral believes he has concluded a clean agreement.

One of the separated dream-soldiers spoke to a Vilefin goblin scout during shared shelter at the southern pass. The dream account — blue corridor, descending, cold light — has now left Lostfeld's walls. Ironmaul recalled the soldier and placed him in medical isolation. Varek camps 4 hours from the gate.

Velorn clan guards repositioned quietly following the clan head's meeting with the Arkenor lieutenant. Lythara sent a second demand message to the lieutenant, still unaware he disclosed her offer. She is pressing toward a trap she cannot see.

Rowen's fleet continues east. He spent the day writing from a document that appeared older than it should be — possibly notes from the chamber below the sinking island's throne room. One captain observed him speaking quietly to himself. The destination is closing.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Proclamation vs Intelligence Leak
active_tensions.description: The Gnome Syndicate now holds confirmed knowledge of the proclamation. The Admiral does not. The Vampires are one inference away. Caeris is hardening his position without knowing who already knows. The next 24 hours will determine who moves first with this information.

active_tensions.factions: Tidefall — Retainer Clause vs Admiral's Ignorance
active_tensions.description: The harbor-treaty is signed and the Dur Khadur retainer clause is active. The Admiral believes he concluded a clean agreement. Dur Khadur's unit is at readiness. A formal succession uncertainty declaration by anyone triggers autonomous mercenary deployment.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Dream Contagion Beyond the Walls
active_tensions.description: The Entity's dream has passed outside Lostfeld through a separated soldier's goblin contact. The isolation protocol has already failed at its edges. Varek arrives tomorrow. Whether he arrives in time to contain the spread is an open question.

Lythara is escalating pressure on the Arkenor lieutenant without knowing he already burned her channel to Velorn. Velorn is mobilizing guards. When Lythara's next action reaches Velorn, she will be striking an alerted target who has already moved to defend.

character_updates.name: Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Woke from the Tidefall throne dream unsettled. Ordered garrison accountability without explaining why. Drafting a conditional public announcement. He is moving as if expecting pressure from a direction he cannot name.

character_updates.name: Seer
character_updates.status: Noticed the garrison accountability order and read it as a fortification signal. Drafting a private note of uncertain destination. He does not know about the dream but is reading its consequences in the palace's movement.

character_updates.name: Admiral (Tidefall)
character_updates.status: Countersigned the harbor-treaty with confidence. Sent a rider to Twin Cities. Believes his coalition is legally consolidated. Does not know about the proclamation, the retainer clause's implications, or Dur Khadur's readiness status.

character_updates.name: Grand Druid Varek
character_updates.status: Camped 4 hours from Lostfeld's gate. Reviewed the sealed iron case contents alone before resealing. Runner dispatched. He will arrive at first light tomorrow. He has seen what is in the case and his expression when he resealed it was difficult to read.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara
character_updates.status: Sent a second demand to the Arkenor lieutenant. Still unaware of the Velorn disclosure. She believes she is applying pressure to a hesitating agent. She is applying pressure to a trap.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: Spent the day writing from a document of uncertain age. Emerged at dusk to confirm heading. One captain observed him speaking quietly to himself. He is integrating something large and moving east with it.

faction_morale.reason: Caeris is moving with purpose, but the garrison order without explanation is beginning to produce low-level unease among officers who are not told the reason.
The Admiral's signing ceremony felt like a victory. The fleet is in order. The morale is built on incomplete information and will not survive contact with what the Gnomes are holding.

The news that a dream-soldier spoke to an outsider before recall has deepened the sense that containment is impossible. Ironmaul is visibly exhausted. The garrison is watching him for signs of collapse.
The Velorn clan head is mobilizing guards unseen by Lythara. The fracture is hardening below the surface. When it becomes visible it will be sudden.
They hold the succession's most valuable intelligence and are in no rush. Patience is a Gnome virtue and they are practicing it with satisfaction.
Varek's arrival is imminent. The mission is clear. The Conclave believes it is moving toward something that will justify the long journey and the sealed case.
They are one inference from the proclamation intelligence. The operative is calm and close. The wavering lord is deepening in their influence daily.

The retainer clause is active and the advance unit is at readiness. They are positioned to move before any competitor understands the legal trigger has been pulled. Anticipation of advantage is its own form of morale.

faction_resources.pressure: Stable, with no acute resource shortages.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.

seer_journey.last_outcome: Noticed the garrison accountability order and read it as a fortification signal. Drafting a private note of uncertain destination. He does not know about the dream but is reading its consequences in the palace's movement.
